the manner of this cover being to be measure by the custom of the nation . partly by a argument à pari , from man ; for who , even themselves be judge , it will be a uncomely thing to wear a veil , that be , a woman habit : so , by the like reason , be it as uncomely and absurd for a woman to be without a veil , that be , in the guise and dress of a man . and howsoever the devil of the gentile sometime take pleasure in uncomeliness and absurd garb and gesture ; yet the god who they worship , and his holy angel who be present at their devotion , love a comely accommodation , agreeable to nature and custom , in such as worship he . for this cause therefore ( say he Vers. 10. ) ought a woman to have a cover on her head , because of the angel . last , he conclude it from the example and custom both of the jewish and christian church ; neither of which have any such use , for their woman to be unveil in their sacred assembly : if any man ( say he Vers. 16. ) be contentious , ( that be , will not be satisfy with these reason ) let he know that we ( that be we of the circumcision ) have no such custom , nor the church of God . for so , with saint Ambrose , Anselme , and some of the ancient , i take the meaning of the apostle to be in those word . thus you have hear brief , what be the fault of these corinthian dame , which the apostle here taxe . from which we our self may learn thus much , that God require a decent and comely accommodation in his house in the act of his worship and service : for if in their habit and dress , sure much more in their gesture and deportment , he love nothing that be unseemly in the one or in the other : which i doubt some of we , at least of the young sort , be not so observant of in this place as we shall ; and therefore with those who it concern will amend it . and thus i conclude my discourse .

which without question be not till after Joshua be dead and bury , 1 Sam 4. and be usual understand of that time when the ark be take captive by the philistine . and yet be not only here a sanctuary mention at Sichem , but in the begin of the chapter , the elder and officer of the tribe be say , upon Joshua be summons , i•s . 24. • . to have present themselves there before the lord ; which speech use to imply as much . if we say the ark of God be take out of its place at Shiloh , and bring to Sichem by the Levite , Ios. 24. 26. upon occasion of this general assembly ; yet the difficulty will not be remove . for first , how can the Ark alone give denomination to the place where it stand , to be call the sanctuary of the lord ; or second , if the altar be there with it , Deut. 16. 21 , 22. how be the law of God observe , which say , thou shall n•t plant a grove of any tree ( or any tree ) near unto the altar of the lord thy god , which thou shall make thou ; neither shall thou set up a pillar , which the lord thy God hate : whenas here be both , a oak or Quercetum in the sanctuary of God , and a pillar or statue erect under it ? third , this sanctuary , whatsoever it be , must be something which have a constant and fix station , and be not temporary and mutable ; and that , because the oak , under which this pillar be erect by Joshua , be here design and point out by it as by a constant and stand mark : else to what purpose have it be to sign out the oak by it , if it be such as will be here to day and not to morrow ?
